entry.title: # Switiching from Classis MRP(MD01/MD02) to MRP Live
entry.content:
Hello all

Since we are migrating from ECC to S/4HANA, Classic MRP will transition to MRP Live. We will continue using Classic MRP for one more year before fully adopting MRP Live. In this context, where will the data for customizing reports for MRP be stored during the period of Classic MRP, and what will happen to the old data once MRP Live is implemented? Will it be retained or will it vanish?"   

 can anyone help in above case.

Please Reply 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Learning new UX - blank Catalog Search no longer avai...
entry.content: Dear,

entry.title: No printout for FINT cancellation invoice
entry.content:
Hi

When cancelling an interest invoice using FINTSHOW, a credit printout of the cancellation invoice should be generated, as it is a legal requirement to send the customer a credit invoice.

However, FINTSHOW only generates the cancellation document and doesn't print it. It is not possible to use the reprint function for the cancellation invoice, since it is not displayed in FINTSHOW.

Thanks

entry.title: How to increase length of domain 'SETVAL' from char24->char40?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
How to increase length of 'SETVAL' domain char24->char40?
It's used to convert material to LMN at SAP generated t-codes like 'S_ALR....'.
U can find it at RGSMH-MIN, RGSMH-MAX.
Can't find any note how to do it.

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Invoice Scanning
entry.content:
Hi @stefan_resag 
thanks for your insights.

I have the same question as Jonas above:
However when we try to upload an invoice via Odata, the invoice scanning remains in the status 'Scanning in Process'.
We are using a technical user which is assigned to the invoice scanning workcenter.
When we upload using user credentials, the scanning works fine.
Can you please advice on how to proceed?

Thanks!
BR
Simon

entry.title: Re: How to increase length of domain 'SETVAL' from char24->char40?
entry.content:
To change a type of standard object which doesn't have a dedicated tool for that, you need to get an object modification key and modify directly the object. You will be asked the key while changing the object, and a task of type repair will be created in your workbench transport request. Of course, changing the length of a domain is very risky because it may have many present and future impacts, you may not see/test all of them.

EDIT: only very experienced ABAP developers should be assigned to this type of task.

how can i do?

Hi Tâm_Nguyễn,

Try running Fiddler tool to capture the request and response.

It will give you more precise information about the issue.

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Hybrid Connector - Classic vs New Product compliance solution

It surely is underdeveloped (and almost useless) in SAP S/4HANA for PC on-prem (and private cloud). For companies that deal with substances with "legal amount limitations" (I don't know how to put this better in English) SVT is fundamental tool...

Stocktake Reconciliation Program

Stocktake Reconciliation Table

Anyone has any clue what's the functionality or concept behind these? 

Appreciate any help or clue.

Regards, Sivasankaran S

entry.title: Re: How to increase length of domain 'SETVAL' from char24->char40?
entry.content:
There's no OSS notes of changing it?
I think it's should be connected with KKB_MATNR and others LMN add-ons.
I have problem at mine S_ALR_87013047 report, when material > 24 symbols i'm getting an dump.

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content:
Hi Nami,

I see and understand now. Let me tell you that SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool is not a tool for data migration, the focus of this tool is data model conversion, please check the community I was pointing before, there are 2 documents there (what this tool is and the brief) that describe very well the capabilities of the tool. Those docs there explain that the data migration part is out of the scope of this tool and we only allow some partial support generating script for Hana on-prem using SDA to move the data, for example:

   You need to look for another way to move the data, a couple of possibilities can be Hana SDI and also direct export/import to/from text files.

 

    Regards.

    Javier.

entry.title: Re: Using Typescript in the CAP Project
entry.content: For those who are looking for answer to similar problem. i did not find a solution. The work around i did is to run the tsc command to convert the ts files to javascript and then continue with debugging.

entry.title: Re: How to increase length of domain 'SETVAL' from char24->char40?
entry.content: The concept of modification keys is abandoned in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Latest Integration Flow Design Guidelines for SAP Integration Suite – shipments in Q3 & Q4 2020
entry.content:
Hello,

the Webshop used in the "Integration Flow Design Guidelines" exercises and in this SAP tutorial  are actually empty and the OData Service was open in the past...now we have to authenticate with SAP User and after login the response is "not found".

So actually the most of the exercises could not be used, because they did not work without the webshop and its odata service.

Are there changes planned to change the excersises or will the webshop work again in a short time.

By now my/our training "stops", caused by the not working webshop...

 

Thank you 

 

 Regards 

 Thorsten

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

I've been hearing from friends about the promising career opportunities in SAP, which has sparked my curiosity as I've been considering a career change for a while. Recently, I came across a post on a blogger specifies in SAP [https://www.khanhnph.com/] , which brought me here. I’m eager to learn SAP and am ready to invest the time needed to master all SAP fundamentals and business processes. According to Vietnam job market recent trend, I believe there's much to learn and significant potential for growth.

Nam

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi Mr. David,

I hope you're doing well. I’ve been exploring career opportunities in SAP, and after doing some research, I’ve realized how valuable SAP certifications can be in advancing my career. I am very interested in taking the SAP certification exam, as I believe it will equip me with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in this field. I’m eager to dive deep into the SAP ecosystem and demonstrate my commitment by achieving this certification. Any guidance or advice you can offer would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you and Best Regards,

Phat.

entry.title: Re: How to increase length of domain 'SETVAL' from char24->char40?
entry.content:
Hello @shiz0frenik 

SETVAL is already 40 chars long in S/4HANA:

You can modify the length of the domain, just change the object. However be aware that such a change will cause subsequent activation of all dependent objects, i.e. 23 data elements in which SETVAL domain is used and then all tables and structures where these 23 data elements are used. On top of that all reports that used the reactivated data dictionary objects will be activated again. It is a risky operation!

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Invoice cannot release accounting document.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in September
entry.content:
Another great month comes to an end, and we delivered great new features with our two September releases. There are also lots of excellent new blogs in the community and top features on YouTube below.

 

My top features in September

Cloud object stores as sources in replication flows (HDLFS, AWS S3, GCS, ADLS2)

We already offered cloud storage providers as targets in replication flows and with release 2024.19 you can now use the following cloud storage providers and the corresponding connection types to add sources to replication flows:

  • Data lake Files from SAP HANA Cloud, data lake
  • Amazon Simple Storage Service
  • Google Cloud Storage
  • Microsoft Azure Data Lake Gen2

More information. 

Remove persisted data in task chains

When creating a task chain, the task chain editor now lets you choose to remove the replicated or persisted data for remote tables and views. 
More information.

Scheduling mass deletion of filtered records from local tables

From the Local Tables monitor, you can clean up your table records on-demand. You can delete all records contained in the table, delete records using filter conditions also using schedules, or delete records marked as deleted in your local table where delta capture is enabled. 
More information.

Analytic Model: define order of variables

You can now change the order of variables in the editor of an analytic model using drag & drop. This order will be taken into account in the data preview and in the prompt in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Changing space language for metadata translation

Earlier in the year we introduced the translation dashboard to support metadata translation. You can now change the source language of a Space which is enabled for translation for example from German to English, and the German translations are kept after the change. Be aware, that if you reset the translation setting to "not selected" all translations will be removed. 
More information.

 

 

SAP TechEd 2024

End of September - one week away from SAP TechEd 2024 which fully virtual this year. All sessions will be streamed live from the App-house in Heidelberg as a free online experience on October 8th and 9th 2024. There are multiple SAP Datasphere sessions at virtual TechEd, so feel free to join them live:

Track Overview

  • DA100 – Data in the age of AI

Lectures

  • DA106 – Integration of SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere solutions
  • DA107 – SAP Datasphere: Recent innovations, road map, and strategy
  • DA110 – How to treat data as a product
  • DA195 – DataRobot and SAP Datasphere: Rapidly build AI-powered applications
  • DA200 – Best of SAP BW in SAP Datasphere
  • DA201 – Zalando Payments' journey to unlock the value of information assets

Jump-Start

  • DA180 – Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end

 

My top blogs in September

@kostertim explains the new feature about release states with compatibility contracts in his blog 
➡️ A deeper look at the Release State Feature in SAP Datasphere

@abange writes about the SAP data and analytics advisory methodology and especially gives more details about the data and analytics maturity assessment 
➡️ Introducing Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment of “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology"

My friend @AndreasForster has written another great blog with his 
➡️ Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP Datasphere

@thorebedey is looking closer into the dynamic filter pushdown capability for remote tables 
➡️ SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown

 

More blogs to check out 👇 

  • Integrating with SAP Datasphere API through BTP Destination Using OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls
  • Transitive attribute in SAP Datasphere
  • The Case for SAP BTP: The People’s Verdict
  • E-Learning: SAP BusinessObjects BI - Hybrid Integration
  • “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
  • Consuming Data from Datasphere to Azure Data Factory via ODBC
  • Easing the transition: 7 steps for a business data fabric deployment with SAP Datasphere
  • Understanding Replication Flow Results in Azure (ADLS Gen2)
  • Introducing Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment of “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology"
  • A deeper look at the Release State Feature in SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere – How-To integrate Open SQL Procedures in a Task Chain
  • SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future
  • Performance Monitoring in SAP Datasphere
  • Maximizing ROI: 4 benefits of a business data fabric with SAP Datasphere
  • Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q3 2024
  • Unlocking Innovation with SAP Business Technology Platform: A Beginners Guide
  • Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
  • Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown
  • Currency conversion in SAP Datasphere
  • Building a business data fabric: SAP Datasphere vs. DIY implementations
  • Streamlining Data Sharing: Emailing CSV Files from SAP Datasphere (DWC) using DIC
  • How to Connect S4HANA Public Cloud With SAP Datasphere
  • Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere
  • Gen AI meets SAP: Improve your sales strategies with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere and SAC

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting. Also check out the new support content for SAP Datasphere on SAP Help for troubleshooting and analysis guides, how-to guides, technical details, and more.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.

entry.content:

I dont think it can be covered by standard solution. For this you need to develop custom program. The logic be behind this we saprate feild name in SAP EWM and non SAP system.  Deveplope a custom program and send them to non sap system build logic as per requirement and it will print label in non sap system

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content:
@nguyen_thanh_hieu That's odd because the Ctrl-Space option just works when you hit Ctrl-Space. i've not seen that work sometimes or never. just always. I've also just created your exact scenario inside a Switch onChange() event and added the same code and then hit Ctrl-Space - and of course it works just fine. 

So questions - does this only fail here or in other places, browsers, stories? Does it work in other stories? Have you tried to logout, close ALL browsers, clear cache and try again? For me this should just work... I assume you have tried Ctrl-Space in other places and it's worked for you?

Let me know...

Daniel.

entry.title: create and update value mapping table based on incoming payload
entry.content:
hello Dear Collogues,

i need to update value mapping dynamically or runtime based on payload.

for example

<contacts>

<name>ram</name>
<email>ram@gmail.com</email>
<depratment>IT</depratment>
<address>abc india </address>
</contacts>

our VM looks like

sourceAgency1 Identi1 targetAgency2 Idernr2


key value
ram,ram@gmail.com,IT 123456(unique number generated)

so next time same key come its not inseted key value in VM table

please help me its very complex for us as i am new one to SAP CPI

entry.title: Seize the Full Value of Web App Security Investment? (Key Metrics You Can’t Avoid)
entry.content:
Did you know that the average cost of a data breach in 2024 has increased by 10% from last year? Now imagine that cost multiplied by the number of vulnerabilities you might not even be aware of. Yet many companies are throwing money at security solutions without a solid strategy, leaving them vulnerable and unsure if their investments are truly effective. Let me be clear, fast-growing businesses can’t afford this uncertainty to sustain their position in the market.   

In this high-speed digital world, risks are evolving just as quickly as our technologies. So, it’s high time for businesses to rethink their approach and take control by embedding the right metrics into their security strategies.  By doing so, firms can’t just proactively defend against threats but also leverage these metrics to gain a competitive advantage.  

As someone who has navigated the shifting sands of tech for over 24+ years, I can assure you that these metrics can be a game-changer for your business. Now is the time to shift your focus from mere outputs – like the number of firewalls or alerts to the outcomes that truly matter. Even studies have shown that businesses with robust security protocols not only mitigate risks but also build trust and drive sustainable revenue growth.  

Let’s dig into the key metrics that you can’t afford to miss while evaluating your web application security performance 

A List of Essential Metrics to Maximize Your Web App Security Value 

When you navigate the complexities of your web application security, these metrics will serve as your compass, guiding you toward meaningful investments and ultimately, a more secure future. By carefully assessing these critical areas, you can transform your security investments into measurable business value. So, roll up your sleeves and turn these insights into actionable strategies:  

  • A Level of Readiness 

With the help of this metric, you can assess your organization’s preparedness to handle security incidents. By evaluating your readiness, you can identify gaps in training, tools, and processes to ensure that your team is agile and equipped to respond confidently. A high readiness level means quicker, more efficient incident responses which will ultimately reducing potential losses. 

  •  An Incident's Frequency and Impact 

It is one of the metrics that businesses often overlook. By closely understanding how often security incidents occur and their severity allows you to prioritize resources more effectively. If you notice an uptick in incidents, it might signal the need for enhanced security measures or training. Moreover, it can help you shift from a reactive to a proactive security posture, significantly lowering the costs associated with breaches. 

  • Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) 

Speed is highly imperative while dealing with cybersecurity. The quicker you can detect anomalies, the better your chances of mitigating damage. That’s where, MTTD can help you discover your efficiency and make it up-to-mark accordingly. A lower MTTD means your security systems are functioning effectively, allowing you to allocate resources more efficiently and reduce the overall impact of incidents on your organization. 

  • Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR) 

Once an incident is detected, how quickly can you resolve it? Do you know a lower MTTR translates to less downtime and a smoother customer experience, which is invaluable for maintaining trust? By strategically evaluating this metric, you can streamline your incident response processes, reducing the financial impact of incidents and enhancing user satisfaction. 

  • Mean Time to Contain (MTTC) 

How long does it take you to stop a threat from spreading? This metric measures how swiftly you can contain a breach. A low MTTC means you can contain the breach and prevent the spread of threats, which is essential for protecting sensitive data. A high MTTC rate means the threat is running wild. By timely monitoring this metric, you can improve your containment strategies and implement measures early-on. In turn, you can ensure even when breaches occur, their impact is limited. 

  • False Positive Rate 

Not all alerts signal a threat. This metric helps you measure the accuracy of your security tools. A high false positive rate can lead to wasted resources and time. On the other hand, if you Keep this rate low you can reduce unnecessary panic and optimize your security team’s focus on real issues. This metric can be a great help to refine your detection tools, ensuring that your team spends time addressing genuine threats rather than chasing false alarms. 

  • Code Coverage 

This metric assesses how much of your code is being tested for vulnerabilities. Higher coverage means fewer blind spots, leading to a more secure web application development. By regularly evaluating code coverage, you can identify areas for improvement in your web app development process, ultimately reducing the risk of security vulnerabilities in your applications. 

  • Return on Security Investment (ROSI) 

Ultimately, you want to see that your investments pay off. ROSI quantifies the financial benefit of your security measures and help you justify your budget and strategy. A high ROSI means your investments are paying off. A low ROSI means you may need to reevaluate your strategies. This way, you can make informed decisions about future investments, ensuring that every dollar spent on security contributes to your bottom line. 

  • Compliance Adherence Rate 

Are you meeting the standards set by regulators? Because staying compliant isn’t just about avoiding fines; it’s about building trust with your customers. That’s where this metric measures your compliance with industry regulations. A strong adherence rate can enhance your brand reputation, making you a preferred choice for customers who prioritize security. On the other hand, a low adherence means you are playing with fire.  

  • Intrusion Attempts 

Monitoring the number of intrusion attempts provides insight into the threatscape. As high number might indicate that you are a target. This way you can understand these patterns and strengthen your defences proactively. Furthermore, you can tailor your security measures to meet the specific threats your organization faces, maximizing your resources and improving overall security posture. 

Build Risk-Resilient Web Apps That Thrive in a Complex Cyber Sphere 

Remember, security is not merely a checkbox – it is the backbone of a reliable web application development services. So, it’s highly imperative to evaluate whether your security measures are truly worthwhile or just a pain point on your investment. 

For that, you can utilize these key metrics as your guide to fine-tune your strategies and ensure that your AppSec investments are not only justified but also integral to your success.  

So, what are you waiting for? Embed these metrics in your development process and create applications that deliver impact while standing resilient against cyber threats. Because a secure web app is the foundation of sustained success.

entry.title: Unable to create material master due to Accounting1 error in MM01
entry.content: Hi Experts, I created a material master by MM01 and the material XXXX was created successfully message was also displayed. The material is created but not updated in any material master table such as MARA, MARD etc. and no record of that material in that plant and location. After successfully creation of material  error " Express document update was cancelled recieved from the author XXXXX" was poped up. Then I created the material with basic tabs only and extended the material to all views one by one and I found thta the error is due to some configration mismatching in Accounting1 view. Even though my material ledger is active but unabale to save the material due to Accounting1 view. Please guide me to solve the issues.

entry.title: Re: Invoice cannot release accounting document.
entry.content: Yes, I get the error from pricing analysis. But I would like to know why the other invoice can be released to accounting with no price. When I checked pricing analysis for the invoice which could be released, it was shown that pricing conditions were not accessed due to the requirement not being met.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget: QRC3 2024 Release
entry.content:
Purpose

This blog introduces the latest updates of Custom Widgets features within SAP Analytics Cloud, as part of the QRC3 2024 release.

Overview

1. Custom Widget: Showing unbooked data values

Previously, the query result returned for the custom widget doesn't include unbooked data. However, displaying unbooked values is crucial. Hence, we're introducing this feature that incorporates unbooked data in the query results which can be utilized by custom widgets. this enhancement allows customers to effectively use unbooked data in their custom widgets.

2. Custom Widget: Support for calculated and account-based dimensions

In the optimized story experience, you can now seamlessly create and utilize calculated or account-based dimensions for custom widgets. This feature is essential for customers who wish to combine dimensions, group them, or create account-based ones for use in custom widgets.

3. Custom Widget: Increasing the number of the hosted custom widget in SAC to 50

Beforehand, SAC user was able to host until 25 Custom Widget per tenant, with the QRC3 release we increased the number to 50 in order to offer more flexibility for custom widget developer to extend their visualisation and easily hosted into SAC and into their Stories.

For further information, visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. 

Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: SAP BTP RAP managed batch service is failing due to duplicate keys
entry.content:
I have created RAP MANAGED service for batch processing via API gateway service to post data into a custom table in SAP, the problem is like service is getting failed if the subsequent request has duplicate records when compared to custom table entries which had already existed in the table.

Note: If no duplicates service is up and posting the data.

Any suggestion to resolve this issue is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Onboarding: The New "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding continues to evolve, providing organizations with robust tools to ensure a seamless and efficient onboarding experience. One of the latest enhancements is the introduction of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in the Process Variant Manager (PVM). This feature allows organizations to better organize, streamline, and customize tasks for new hires, ensuring a smoother transition into their roles.

Now with the Upcoming 2H 2024 Release, you can configure the Primary New Hire Tasks and Secondary New Hire Tasks steps to customize where standard and custom tasks appear in the Onboarding program. You can also attach a Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step to either of these steps.

The "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step: An Overview

The introduction of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step offers an enhanced way to manage tasks for new hires. This feature differentiates between essential tasks (Primary Tasks) that are crucial for all new hires and supplementary tasks (Secondary Tasks) that may only be relevant for specific groups or roles.

Key Benefits of the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step

  1. Enhanced Task Management and Prioritization:
    • By categorizing tasks as either Primary or Secondary, HR teams can ensure that new hires prioritize critical activities first. This helps streamline the onboarding process, reducing confusion and ensuring compliance with mandatory requirements.
  2. Customized Onboarding Experience:
    • Secondary tasks allow for a more tailored onboarding experience, catering to the unique needs of different employee groups. This ensures that new hires receive relevant information and training specific to their roles, increasing engagement and readiness for the job.
  3. Flexibility and Scalability:
    • The ability to configure tasks in the PVM based on various criteria, such as location, department, or role, provides HR teams with the flexibility to adapt the onboarding process to evolving business needs. It also allows for easy scalability as organizations grow and expand.
  4. Improved Compliance and Reporting:
    • With a clear distinction between mandatory and optional tasks, HR teams can easily track and report on task completion. This ensures compliance with legal and organizational requirements and provides visibility into the onboarding progress of new hires.

Prerequisites

  • A process variant flow for your Onboarding program that includes a Primary New Hires Tasks step and a Secondary New Hire Tasks step.
  • A custom task that you want to add to your Onboarding process variant.
  • Configure a business rule scenario for the process variant.

Configuring the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" in PVM

To configure the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in the PVM, follow these steps:

Create or Edit a Process Variant:

You can either create a new process variant or edit an existing one. Select the relevant process variant for which you want to configure the new hire tasks.

Add the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" Step:

Drag and drop the "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step into the desired location within the process flow. This step can be placed at any point in the onboarding process, depending on when you want these tasks to be presented to the new hire.

Configure Primary and Secondary Tasks:

To add standard tasks to the Primary New Hire Tasks step:

  1. Go to Admin Center>Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks.
  2. Select the  Edit icon next to the Onboarding program. The Manage Onboarding Program page displays.
  3. Select + Add Task or + Add a Custom Task and choose a task from the dropdown menu.
  4. For the Required task field, select Yes or No.
  5. For the Assigned to field, select a name from the dropdown menu.
  6. Select Done to save. The task is added to the Primary New Hire Task section.

To add a task to the Secondary New Hire Tasks section, select the task and drag it to the Secondary New Hire Tasks section. The order of the tasks in this section is the order in which they appear in the Onboarding program. You can select a task and drag it to the desired order within the program.

You can also attach a New Hire Tasks Document Flow step to either the Primary New Hire Tasks step or the Secondary New Hire Tasks step to collect signatures for any custom task. Custom tasks requiring a signature must be added to the new hire task step (either primary or secondary) and attached to the Document Flow for New Hire Tasks. The Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step is triggered upon completion of the custom tasks assigned in this step.

If you attached a Document Flow for the New Hire Tasks step to either the Primary New Hire Tasks step or the Secondary New Hire Tasks step, you need to adjust the Assign Forms (OnboardingAssignFormRule) rule to ensure the forms are assigned based on the step with the attached Document Flow for New Hire Tasks.

Save and Deploy the Process Variant:

Once the configuration is complete, save and deploy the process variant. The new hire tasks will now be presented according to the defined steps and categories during the onboarding process.

Conclusion

The "Primary and Secondary New Hire Tasks" step in SuccessFactors Onboarding represents a significant enhancement in how organizations can manage and personalize the onboarding process. By categorizing tasks into Primary and Secondary, HR teams can ensure that new hires receive the right information and complete essential activities promptly. This not only improves the onboarding experience for new employees but also helps organizations maintain compliance and drive engagement from day one.

Note: The Secondary New Hire Tasks steps can’t be added before the Personal Data Collection step, after the Final Review Step, or after the Primary New Hire Tasks step.

entry.title: Build the Skills to Bring out Your Best - Time to board the SAP TechEd
entry.content:
Register now for SAP TechEd Virtual, on October 8 – 9, and get a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub!
Prepare for this year’s event with free learning journeys, demos, basic trials, and hands-on use cases on the hottest topics on SAP TechEd tracks.
Don’t miss out on this great opportunity to expand your skills and steam ahead in your career.

Start learning                              Register now!

entry.title: SAP Business AI Cheat Sheet – Your Quick Reference Guide
entry.content:
In this blog, I am going to share my personal cheat sheet related to various AI topics in the context of SAP. This cheat sheet is the collection of most useful blogs, help documents, courses, and more on SAP Business AI, Embedded AI, Joule, Generative AI Hub, HANA vector engine, SAP AI Core etc., which I’ve crafted over time.

Don't forget to bookmark this page for future reference 😉

First, let’s have a quick glimpse on various components and terminologies in SAP Business AI.

 

Note: If you are looking for a simple, step-by-step guide to learn SAP Business AI, you may refer to Generative AI with SAP Blog Series.

If you are new to Generative AI, I will  recommend you to spend few minutes reading the blog series Generative AI for Beginners and 21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified.

 

SAP Business AI

SAP Business AI Product Page

SAP AI Community

FREE Course - Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

FREE Course - Generative AI at SAP

Roadmap

SAP Business AI Whitepaper

Get Started with SAP Business AI

 

Joule

Joule Product Page

Joule Info Sheet

Joule Community

Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI

Explore Joule in SAP Ariba Category Management

Explore Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Explore Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud

Explore Joule in SAP SuccessFactors 

Explore Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Explore Joule in SAP BTP

Joule Help Page

 

Embedded AI in SAP Solutions

Embedding AI in SAP solutions - Overview

SAP Business AI in ERP and finance

SAP Business AI in supply chain

SAP Business AI in HR

SAP Business AI in procurement

SAP Business AI in sales & service

SAP Business AI in marketing & commerce

SAP Business AI in industries

SAP Business AI in IT and platform

 

SAP AI Services

SAP AI Services Community

Document Information Extraction

Data Attribute Recommendation

Personalized Recommendation

Get Started with Document Information Extraction and Generative AI

Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Business Documents and Enrich Data

Use Pre-Trained Machine Learning Models to Process Unstructured Text

Use Machine Learning to Get Recommendations Based on Users' Browsing History

Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents

Use Pre-Trained Machine Learning Models to Process Unstructured Text

Enrich your master and transactional data with AI-based data attribute recommendations

 

AI Foundation

Get started with AI Foundation

Getting Familiar with AI Foundation

AI Foundation on SAP BTP: Q1 2024 Release Highlights

AI Foundation, SAP’s all-in-one AI toolkit for developers

SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation

Unlock the Full Potential of Artificial Intelligence with the AI Developer for SAP

 

SAP AI Core

SAP AI Core Community

FREE Course - Learning how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP BTP

Hands-on: Predictive AI with SAP AI Core

How SAP's Generative AI Architecture Redefines Business Applications

SAP AI Core Help Page

Generative AI with SAP AI Core

Predictive AI with SAP AI Core

SAP AI Core SDK

 

SAP AI Launchpad

SAP AI Launchpad Community

SAP AI Launchpad Help Page

SAP AI Launchpad Tutorials

 

Generative AI Hub

Starter kit for generative AI hub 

Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core

Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Launchpad

How SAP’s Generative AI Hub facilitates embedded, trustworthy, and reliable AI

Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad

SAP generative AI hub SDK

Set Up SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub

 

SAP HANA Cloud vector engine

SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine: Quick FAQ Reference

Harnessing Generative AI Capabilities with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Vector Engine Guide

Vectorize your Data : SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Engine for Unified Data Excellence

 

SAP Foundation Model

Leveraging Generative AI Advancements for Linked Business Data

 

SAP AI Units

SAP AI Services List

AI Unit Estimator

Overview of SAP AI Units

 

SAP AI Ethics

Responsible AI in SAP

SAP AI Ethics Handbook

SAP Global AI Ethics Policy

SAP Trust Center

 

Reference Architectures

Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP

Federated Machine Learning

Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)

 

Liked it? Don't forget to give a kudos!

Hope this cheat sheet will be helpful for you. If I have missed anything important, please let me know in comment 🙂

entry.title: Block for accounting of additions of an AF with zero useful life and zero residual value
entry.content:
Dear support
I need to know if there is any parameterization at the customizing level to block postings for additions to Fixed Assets with zero useful life and zero residual value.
I was checking and couldn't find any standard options.
Could you tell me if this is possible using the BADI FIAA_002? Or is there another way to develop ABAP logic to solve this requirement of my company?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Block for accounting of additions of an AF with zero...
entry.content:
Blocking certain assets from  posting may be achieved by validation rules.

You may find further information in Logical Field Group Assignment in Validation and Substitution Rules

Alternatively, an ABAP report shall be created by the local SAP Consultant.

entry.title: Re: The serial number is missing linked at the product
entry.content: solution: The stock was written off and upload again.

entry.title: Discussion forum closed
entry.content:
Dear learners, 

We hope you enjoyed the course "Modernizing Integration with SAP Integration Suite".

The discussion forum for this course is now closed and will no longer be moderated. We’d like to thank everyone for submitting their questions, suggestions, and feedback.

As mentioned at the start of the course, the following channels will remain open for you to provide feedback:

  • You can provide feedback on lesson level using the smileys below the videos in the course. 
  • For technical and general questions, use the Feedback function in the top left corner of the learning page.

See you in the next SAP Learning MOOC.

Best regards,
Jayanthi
SAP Learning team

entry.content:
Hi, Expert!

When cancelling a Service Contract, you select a reason for cancellation.

Please check.

Soma

entry.title: Re: Load Balancing
entry.content:
Firstly, the WEBDISPATCHER is only used for the HTTP/HTTP connections. For RFC or SAP GUI ones, the Message Server natively handles them (if the LOGON SERVERS groups are configured correctly, TCODE SMLG).

By default, SAP WD uses a Weighted Round Robin strategy by default. But if you see that's not working, you can change it to Load Balancing Using Server Response Times (namely if the servers are very different in performance). Please check this link: 

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw74/7.4.16/en-us/48/8fe37933114e6fe10000000a421937/frameset.htm

entry.title: Issue when publishing content from WPB/SEN
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Background

  • For the last week, When we review the content from the manager (before publishing), all groups/attachments, etc., are visible.
  • The moment we click on the "publish" button/set the publish tick box within the producer, the published view does not show the groups and the attached files are disruptive
  • I have noticed that the /~tag/published/ setting is pulling in an additional / so it//~tag/published/, which looks like that causes the issue

 

Question - Do you know where to identify this and fix it

(Screens of what it should look like and what it is currently doing are below)

What is actually happening when clicking on the "Publish all objects" button within the manager

What it should look like (when we add the /~tag/published tag to the URL after clicking on the "library" button within the manager)

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: production order good receipts is done material is no...
entry.content:
Greetings,

its a tricky question. You can confirm activities but not quantities (quantities depend on OPK4). There is a big difference between confirmation and confirmation with goods movements.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 SAP Forms by Adobe
entry.content:
Hello Sandra, hello Peter,

I have the following additional questions for your presentation and about the "future" of ADS - background of my questions is that we use an ADS System on premise based on JAVA Netweaver:

  • For an S/4 2023 system ADS runs on S/4 HANA JAVA? is this the right understanding?
    is "S/4 HANA JAVA" "only" a new branding for "Netweaver JAVA AS"? Or is this an other new solution?
  • The solution with XSA will be available with S/4 2025, but inside the support timetable for S/4 2025 - Mr. Paulus from SAP SE say's perhaps in 2026 (DSAG Blog).
    is the solution for ADS on XSA free from additional licensing costs?
  • Where can I find official informations (SAP notes, official SAP pages) for more informations?
    (question: is a DSAG Blog or a community blog official?)

Thanks for your answers.

Best regards

Bernd

entry.title: Where can start learning how to use the performance tools in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will provide you with the knowledge and skills required to be able to successfully manage performance in SAP Analytics Cloud. You will gain insight into SAP Analytics Cloud performance tools and troubleshooting performance issues in SAP Analytics Cloud stories.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to describe the different elements of SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and how they can impact on performance, and the tools used to analyze performance issues. You will be able to create stories using performance best practice, use the performance tools to analyze performance, as well as troubleshoot common performance issues related to SAP Analytics Cloud story design.

Prerequisites

  • Acquiring Basic Scripting Skills to Extend Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Picking of partial HUs from Bulk - rounding
entry.content:
Hi SAP collegues,

I have a following business requirement.

Business stores semifinish materials in WHS mainly in full pallets, mainly on bulk ST (sometimes open ST). Partial pallets are allowed in bulk 1 per bin due to ends of production or returns of not consumed stock from PSA. For business it is impossible to separate partial HUs from full HUs.

Scenario:

Mat 123 stored in bulk ST on a full pallets 21ST/PAL + one partial pallet with 17ST/PAL. Stock in same bin and in one quant.

Alt UOM for full pallets is PAL, alt UOM for partial HU is ST.

Request of that product to be delivered to PSA (request for 45 ST).

My expectation is that I have 1 WT to pick complete partial HU with 17 ST on it, then remaining qty will be rounded up to full pallets and in this case 2 full pallets will be proposed.

Material has active packspec 21st/PAL and in determination I do not have OWHT CCtC.

 

We tried so far:

rounding on WPT 4100 - 4; rounding on ST; different entries in scwm/psastage; removal rules based on QUAN asc + desc; ST determination based on quantity classification (we set quantity classification for ST = 1 and PAL = 2); multiple sort rules, filter and limits on a WOCR; tried to work with preffered UOM as PAL set on mat1.

No success so far. May I ask for any advice please as we run out of ideas. Thanks!
EWM - Master Data , EWM - Basic Functions , EWM - Goods Movement , SAP Extended Warehouse Management, EWM - Warehouse Order Processing

entry.title: Re: Error in creation material master MM01
entry.content:
Hello @Saurabh_2804 

The express message "Express document update was cancelled received from the author XXXXX" means that the update task failed. You can get the details of the failed update task in SM13. Typically update tasks fail with short dumps that can be analyzed in ST22. 

You need to verify the details in SM13 and ST22 to see what the root cause is. Please share those details so I can help more.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Error in creation material master MM01
entry.content: I've responded to the question.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 SAP Forms by Adobe
entry.content:
Hello Peter, Hello Sandra,

Does the use of Cloud ADS under RISE with SAP not pose a Data Compliance issue for clients in sensitive industries (Finances, Defense, Security, ...)?

By default, a RISE with SAP contract comes with a preconfigured ADS tenant that does NOT belong to the client's BTP Global Account. Thus, all forms need to be sent out of the Corporate Network of the client to receive a PDF as a response. That means, that highly sensitive information that resides within the S/4HANA Private Cloud instance, like a Sales Order, are sent outside the Corporate Network just for printing.

Could you please elaborate if my understanding is wrong?

Many thanks and kind regards,

Christian Sosa

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.content:

Need your help to give advice. We have requirement to populate second level manager in job relationship but we would like to have it auto populate instead of user input. Any input how to achieve this? We have position management enabled also.

Thank you,
Regards,

Vonny

entry.title: Re: How to find quickly total volume of data in all DSO's...
entry.content:
This should be part of:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/93bea049296a41508d972e117149f784/443521c7bae848a1e10000000a114a6b.html?locale=en-US

 

Otherwise you can just check the Active tables and Fact tables of Infocubes. But for Infocubes that would not have any use, as InfoCube and Dimension tables have only SID-s and key figure values anyways due to the Star Schema.

Also check:

2761002 Naming convention of BW InfoObject and InfoProvider tables

entry.title: Re: Workflow management | Import failed | WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER destination
entry.content:
Hi Amillanm,
I have the same problem. Could you find a solution for that?
BR,
Moein

entry.title: Re: Can payment be stopped after actual payment run and b...
entry.content:
Hello,

Here's the answer that the experts have provided:

"Depending on the scenario, the following happens after the payment run has occurred:

  • There is no Bank Communication Management (BCM) active (no scope item J78 Advanced Cash Payment): The payment medium is directly generated, and the payment can only be stopped by the Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) tenant.
  • BCM is active and
    • There is auto-approve: The payment medium is directly generated, and the payment can only be stopped by MBC.
    • There is an approval process in place: Only the very first approver can reject the whole payment batch or particular payment Items; those payments that were rejected will not be included in the payment medium.
    • The second to fourth approver can only return the whole payment batch to the first approver to change the decision and to reject the whole payment batch and/or payment Items.
  • When there is MBC, it should always be possible to stop the payment before going to bank.
  • When there is no MBC, the payment medium stays in the Manage Payment Medium app and should be processed manually.
  • When there is no MBC, but 2YM process is active, it is up to the customer to stop the payment in CPI / BTP."

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Cross Company Sales Return Process
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have a business requirement for the cross-company Sales Return Process as outlined below:

SAP provides a suspend valuation facility, but it is not applicable for cross-company sales returns. However, our business requires that stock should not be valuated immediately after posting goods receipt. Instead, material valuation should occur only after quality inspection.

We understand that this is not possible as per the standard process.

Any solutions or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank You 

@Fancy_Zou , @Nina_Kou  , @SAPSupport

entry.title: SAP PS latest tcode for obsolete T codes
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

Can you please help with replacement t code for below transactions as these tcodes are obsolete in S4 HANA.

CJI5,CJI8,CN41N,S_ALR_87013558, CJEN,CJI3N,CN41,CN42,CN43,CN60 AND ENGR.

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Display a List and Allow Form Printing in SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello, Thank you for your response.
I have reviewed the blog you shared, but my question was more about knowing if there's another way that doesn't involve using Forms Service by Adobe. Do you know what the alternatives could be, or is it definitely the only way to meet my requirement?
Thanks!😊
Best regards,
--
DMC

entry.title: Charging a penalty on the Material price for rejected materials
entry.content:
Hi There,

The customer declined certain items due to high delivery charges. After the Customer is credited for the items they no longer want we want to charge a penalty of the amount of 20% on the credited sales items value.

The steps are as follows defined by our Client:

  1. Invoice to a customer;
  2. Credit Memo to the customer for the refused part (100% of the value);
  3. Protocol for a penalty in % of the sales value for the rejected part. (Perhaps this step will be a debit memo request with reference to the credited items (items rejected)

I am not sure how the calculation in the Debit memo can be carried out as 20% of the Material Price (the Materials from the credit memo).

Kind Regards,

Lubomira

entry.title: Re: SAP SLICENSE - Warning Message while Login after we r...
entry.content:
Hello,

Besides posting the warning in the pop up message, as requested by another community member, can you confirm that the "Active hardware key" seen in SLICENSE matches the license you have installed? And does it match the "CUSTOMER KEY" seen at the beginning of the "dev_ms" trace file?

And does the "system number" seen at the top of SLICENSE match the one seen on the license entry, in the table below?

Regards,

Isaías
